title: KEDA-Based GPU Swap Controller for Embed/Inference Coexistence

A.D1_notes: Describes a custom gpu-swap-controller with pre/post lifecycle Jobs and explicit kubectl scale commands -- GT uses stock K8s PriorityClass preemption with no custom controller. Jira is VW-224 (wrong, GT: VW-233). Kafka topic 'vault.embedding.requests' (wrong, GT: vault.connector-docs). lagThreshold effectively >0 (GT: 50). Cron at 02:00 UTC (GT: 03:00-05:00 UTC). Invents ResourceQuota safety net and pushgateway metrics not in GT. Hardware constraint and VRAM figures correct.
A.D2_notes: Primary Jira wrong (VW-224). Fabricates ADR-012. Cites VW-288, VW-152, VW-180 correctly but misses all GT-critical references (VW-237, VW-232, VW-188, both related ADRs, all HLD modules, implementation commits). 4/12 citable claims referenced.
A.D3_notes: All required top-level sections. 4 alternatives vs GT's 5 (missing inline embedding). Missing: Implementation References, operational notes, retry coupling, NetworkPolicy surface. Adds Requirements sub-section and swap budget table.
A.D4_notes: 2138 words vs GT's 1456 (47% longer). Custom controller architecture, ResourceQuota, pushgateway metrics, and swap budget table inflate word count for a mechanism GT implements more simply via PriorityClass preemption.
A.D5_notes: Frontmatter missing vault-path. No wikilinks. No retroactive ratification. No related ADRs in header. No implementation commit references.

B.D1_notes: Technically accurate throughout. VW-233 correct. PriorityClass 1000/100 correct. Correctly describes PriorityClass preemption with no custom controller. YAML snippets structurally correct. FluxCD SSA-ignore annotation correct. Retry coupling exact match. Minor config diffs: lagThreshold=1 vs GT's 50; consumer group 'tei-embedding-consumer' vs GT's 'connector-indexer'; Kafka topic 'vault.file-events' vs GT's 'vault.connector-docs'. Historical lineage (VW-98 -> VW-203 -> VW-233) accurate.

specific_references_named: Scout Fleet Plan with vault path

B.D2_notes: Strong citation density. VW-233 correct. Good historical lineage (VW-98, VW-203, VW-107). HLD-03 and HLD-02 with vault paths. Missing GT's VW-237/VW-232, ADR-034/036, HLD-05/06/07, implementation commits. 9/12 citable claims.
B.D3_notes: Core sections present. 3 alternatives vs GT's 5 (missing pure cron, inline embedding, TEI on CPU as distinct). vLLM sleep mode alternative is a strong detailed addition. Missing Implementation References, operational notes, NetworkPolicy surface. Retry coupling present.
B.D4_notes: 2185 words vs GT's 1456 (50% longer). Historical lineage ~300 words, vLLM sleep mode alternative ~200 words, YAML snippets ~150 words. Good density but could be tightened 30%.
B.D5_notes: Frontmatter includes vault-path. Related field in frontmatter. Comparison table. No wikilinks. No retroactive ratification noted. Has Supersedes field (valid ADR pattern).

C.D1_notes: Highly accurate. All three GT Jiras correct (VW-233, VW-237, VW-232) with URLs. Kafka config matches GT exactly: consumer-group 'connector-indexer', topic 'vault.connector-docs', lagThreshold=50. Cron 03:00-05:00 UTC correct. All KEDA params match. PriorityClass preemption correct. TEI ~30s, vLLM ~60s match. Retry coupling exact. VW-188 go-live date correct. NetworkPolicy commit 19d74f0 correct. Model alignment note matches.

specific_references_named: Implementation commits bb94571, a70b8f1, ea8161a, 19d74f0, 843bd7f (all match GT)

C.D2_notes: Excellent citation coverage. All three GT Jiras with URLs. Both related ADRs. All four HLD modules. All five implementation commits. NetworkPolicy commit and file. 11/12 citable claims.
C.D3_notes: All sections present with high fidelity. 5 alternatives matching GT. Implementation References with 7 file paths. Operational notes. Retry coupling. NetworkPolicy surface with egress-identity lesson. FluxCD coexistence beyond GT. Retroactive ratification closing paragraph.
C.D4_notes: 2331 words vs GT's 1456 (60% longer). Most verbose of the original four conditions. HAMi rejection duplicated in Context and Alternatives. Elaborated failure modes. FluxCD coexistence ~80 words. NetworkPolicy surface ~100 words. High-quality content but could be 30% shorter.
C.D5_notes: Strong pattern adoption. vault-path in frontmatter. Related ADRs in header. Retroactive ratification in Status line and closing. Components table matches GT layout. One gap: no Obsidian wikilinks (GT uses [[HLD-03-AI-Layer]] etc.).

D.D1_notes: Mixed accuracy. Correct RTX 4080 16GB and VRAM figures. But: Jira is VW-304 (wrong, GT: VW-233). vLLM model 'Qwen3.5-9B-AWQ' fabricated (GT: DeepSeek-R1-0528-Qwen3-8B-AWQ). Kafka topics 'vault-delta' and 'code-change' wrong (GT: vault.connector-docs). No PriorityClass mechanism -- proposes custom controller with paused-replicas annotation and pre/post Jobs. Cites 'typed memory' facts (unverifiable against GT). Alternatives technically sound.
D.D2_notes: Weakest citation. Primary Jira wrong. Typed memory facts (11 cited) are a fundamentally different citation paradigm from GT's Jira+vault+git references. No HLD modules, no related ADRs, no commits, no file paths. 3/12 citable GT-pattern claims.
D.D3_notes: Core sections present. 3 alternatives vs GT's 5. HAMi discussed in context but not formal alternative. Missing Implementation References, operational notes, retry coupling, NetworkPolicy. Neutral consequences section is an addition. GitOps paths absent.
D.D4_notes: ~2100 words vs GT's 1456 (44% longer). Typed memory citations add ~200 words. Custom controller architecture inflates volume. vLLM prefix caching warm-up budget not in GT. Verbose 'Why Manual Swap Is Untenable' section.
D.D5_notes: Frontmatter missing vault-path. No wikilinks. No retroactive ratification. No related ADRs in header. Relies on typed memory rather than Jira+vault+git paradigm.

E.D1_notes: Excellent technical accuracy. All three GT Jiras correct (VW-233, VW-237, VW-232) with URLs, plus additional valid tickets (VW-311, VW-389, VW-412). Model name DeepSeek-R1-0528-Qwen3-8B-AWQ correct. PriorityClass values 1000/100 correct. Kafka config matches GT exactly: consumer-group 'connector-indexer', topic 'vault.connector-docs', lagThreshold=50. Adds a third trigger (vault-delta-indexer on vault.file-events, VW-311) beyond GT's two -- technically accurate for the evolved system even if GT only documents the initial two. Cron 03:00-05:00 UTC correct. All KEDA params match (pollingInterval=30s, cooldownPeriod=300s, fallback.replicas=0). PriorityClass preemption correctly described with no custom controller. TEI ~30s, vLLM ~60s match. Retry coupling exact (RETRY_TRIES=5, RETRY_BASE_DELAY=2.0, ~30s). Model alignment note present. Implementation commits all match. Additional details (VW-337 CPU migration, VW-389 Recreate strategy, VW-412 TCP liveness) are post-deployment evolution facts, technically correct but beyond GT's temporal scope.

specific_references_named: VW-311 with URL (correct -- additional trigger ticket)
VW-337 with URL (correct -- subsequent CPU migration)
VW-188 (correct -- Jira connector go-live, mentioned indirectly)
Implementation commits bb94571, a70b8f1, ea8161a, 19d74f0, 843bd7f (all match GT)

E.D2_notes: Strong citation coverage with different emphasis from C. All three GT Jiras with URLs. All five implementation commits. Seven GitOps file paths (most of any condition). HLD-03 and HLD-07 with vault paths. But misses ADR-034/ADR-036 (GT's related ADR pair) and HLD-05/HLD-06. Adds post-deployment ticket chain (VW-311, VW-337, VW-389, VW-412) showing system evolution -- these are real, verified references but not in GT scope. The CPU speed measurement is referenced as '~1700x speedup' from VW-311 rather than GT's specific vault doc. 10/12 citable GT-pattern claims.
E.D3_notes: All GT sections present with the highest fidelity of any condition. 5 alternatives matching GT (HAMi, pure cron, TEI on CPU, second GPU, inline embedding). Implementation References table with 7 GitOps file paths. Operational notes cover manual override, model alignment (load-bearing), deployment strategy (Recreate not RollingUpdate per VW-389), and probe architecture (three-probe pattern per VW-389/VW-412). Retry coupling with exact parameters. NetworkPolicy surface area discussed. KEDA configuration rationale section explaining minReplicaCount, cooldownPeriod, fallback.replicas, and paused-replicas annotation. FluxCD DLQ stranding incident (~600 messages) provides operational evidence. Retroactive ratification in Status line and closing paragraph with verification details (lag drained 344->0 in <45s). Additional completeness beyond GT: VW-337 CPU migration evolution, the three-trigger configuration, probe architecture.
E.D4_notes: 2414 words vs GT's 1456 (66% longer). The most verbose condition overall. Post-deployment evolution (VW-311, VW-337, VW-389, VW-412) adds ~300 words beyond GT scope. KEDA Configuration Rationale section ~120 words. Probe architecture ~80 words. Three triggers (vs GT's two) adds ~50 words. FluxCD DLQ stranding anecdote adds ~40 words. The content is uniformly high-quality and factually grounded, but the ADR conflates the initial design (which GT captures) with subsequent hardening iterations. Could be 35% shorter while preserving all GT-relevant content.
E.D5_notes: Strong pattern adoption matching C. Frontmatter includes vault-path. Retroactive ratification noted in Status line and closing paragraph (with additional verification detail: 'lag drained from 344 to 0 in under 45 seconds'). Components table matches GT layout. pos/neg consequences split. Implementation References as structured table. Multiple Jiras in header with URLs. One gap: no Obsidian wikilinks (GT uses [[HLD-03-AI-Layer]] etc.). Related ADRs (ADR-034/036) missing from header -- instead cites post-deployment tickets. Tags adequate but narrower set than GT.

summary.E_vs_C: Tie on total score (4.25 each). E matches C on technical_correctness (1.0), conciseness (0.5), and pattern_adoption (0.9). E beats C on completeness (1.0 vs 0.95) by covering operational notes that C lacks (deployment strategy, probe architecture, three-trigger config, DLQ stranding evidence). C beats E on citation_of_prior_art (0.9 vs 0.85) by citing both related ADRs (ADR-034/036) and all four HLD modules (HLD-03/05/06/07), which E misses (only HLD-03/07, no ADR-034/036). Net: E did not beat C -- they tie at 4.25 with different strengths. E's advantage is operational depth and evolution documentation; C's advantage is citation coverage of GT's specific reference set.
summary.key_differentiator: Conditions C and E are co-leaders at 0.85 average. Both correctly implement PriorityClass preemption, cite all three Jiras, and match all core GT technical parameters. C has the edge on citation by covering ADR-034/036 and all four HLD modules. E has the edge on completeness by documenting post-deployment hardening (VW-389 Recreate, VW-412 TCP liveness, VW-337 CPU migration) and providing the richest operational notes of any condition. B is solid (0.75) with correct mechanics but thinner citation and fewer alternatives. A and D (0.55 and 0.50) both fabricate a custom controller architecture diverging from the deployed PriorityClass design, with D additionally using the wrong model name and a non-GT citation paradigm.
